title: Using a Password Manager
drDigitalIntro: A password manager is an app that remembers all your passwords for you. You only need to remember one master password. The manager creates strong, unique passwords for each site and fills them in automatically. Examples include Apple Keychain, 1Password, and Bitwarden. Using a password manager is much safer than reusing the same simple password everywhere.

playgroundTask.question: What is the main benefit of using a password manager?

options.text: It lets you use the same simple password for all your accounts
It remembers all your unique, strong passwords so you don't have to
It automatically deletes your passwords after one week
It shares your passwords with your family members

drDigitalSuccess: Exactly! A password manager stores all your complex, unique passwords — you just need to remember one master password to unlock them all.
drDigitalHint: Think about what problem having lots of different complex passwords creates, and how a manager solves it.
